commit_message:
remove settle_timeout from Channel state and events

To do this, we moved the old re-use of `channel.settle_timeout` to its
own mapping, saving gas as well since this is only required upon
uncooperative close.
adapt tests to getChannelInfo returning only state

improve gas handling for Arbitrum based networks

First of all, it is not necessary to programmatically set a fixed gas price
value when detecting an Arbitrum network. The gas price gets already set by
a commandline parameter and does not need any further doing. The default 5 Gwei
are enough for the current prices (test network). Else the user can/must set the
value higher.
As the deployment tools are meant to run by hand and usually a developer of the
team, it is not necessary to add any special gas price strategies as we do for
the Raiden services. But to support the correct/better usage, the deployment
documentation got extended for Arbitrum.

move user deposit constant into constructor

The `withdraw_timeout` is a constant value in the `UserDeposit` contract.
Recently its meaning has changed from block numbers to time in seconds. This
brings new challenges to test environments. While is was fairly easy to mine
a couple of empty blocks, manipulating time is a different story. Therefore is
has been decided to make this value configurable during deployment. This works
the same as it does for the `settle_timeout` on the `TokenNetwork` and
`TokenNetworkRegistry` contracts.

GrandaMento - Add hardcoded max for veto period - L07 (#8368)

### Description

Addresses #8330, which calls out that hardcoded min/max values for the veto period would be useful.

* After chatting with Albert, we think there is value in a hardcoded maximum value (set to 4 weeks), but want to preserve the flexibility of being able to set the veto period as low as 0. If down the line the community were to feel that the multisig is not necessary and the `approver` should instead be set to Governance directly, setting the veto period to 0 is desirable. So this PR just adds a `require` enforcing `vetoPeriodSeconds` as <= 4 weeks.
* Apparently there were no existing tests for `setVetoPeriodSeconds`, so they were added

commit 0b51c6576e2d6697b7bc31dda5e969df767151d6
Author: Martin <martin@clabs.co>
Date:   Fri Mar 11 11:09:29 2022 +0100

    Remove oracle check (#9367)

    ### Description

    A check in the Reserve's `addToken` function appeared to be a blocker for getting quicker, single-proposal stable token deployments. The check may have been important in the past, back when the Reserve was responsible for minting/burning stable tokens, and the `isToken` mapping set in the `addToken` function was used for permissioning these behaviors. However, looking at both the monorepo contracts and celo-blockchain, the `isToken` mapping and `_tokens` list of tokens are not used anywhere, thus the check is safe to remove. See [here](https://github.com/celo-org/celo-monorepo/issues/9195#issuecomment-1057942027) for more details.

    ### Tested

    Tests still pass.

    ### Related issues

    - Fixes #9197

Author: Martin <martin@clabs.co>
Date:   Fri Mar 11 11:09:29 2022 +0100

    Remove oracle check (#9367)

    ### Description

    A check in the Reserve's `addToken` function appeared to be a blocker for getting quicker, single-proposal stable token deployments. The check may have been important in the past, back when the Reserve was responsible for minting/burning stable tokens, and the `isToken` mapping set in the `addToken` function was used for permissioning these behaviors. However, looking at both the monorepo contracts and celo-blockchain, the `isToken` mapping and `_tokens` list of tokens are not used anywhere, thus the check is safe to remove. See [here](https://github.com/celo-org/celo-monorepo/issues/9195#issuecomment-1057942027) for more details.

    ### Tested

    Tests still pass.

    ### Related issues

    - Fixes #9197

Remove oracle check (#9367)

### Description

A check in the Reserve's `addToken` function appeared to be a blocker for getting quicker, single-proposal stable token deployments. The check may have been important in the past, back when the Reserve was responsible for minting/burning stable tokens, and the `isToken` mapping set in the `addToken` function was used for permissioning these behaviors. However, looking at both the monorepo contracts and celo-blockchain, the `isToken` mapping and `_tokens` list of tokens are not used anywhere, thus the check is safe to remove. See [here](https://github.com/celo-org/celo-monorepo/issues/9195#issuecomment-1057942027) for more details.

### Tested

Tests still pass.

fix(scripts:artifacts): uses `deployedBytecode` instead of `bytecode`

[EIP-170](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-170) puts "a hard cap on the size of an object that can be saved to the blockchain".

Since it's the deployedBytecode that will be stored in the smart contract, and not the initialization bytecode, we should be looking at the size of the deployedBytecode.

That might also explain (parts of) why the on-chain calculation is slightly different, since that uses the eth_getCode RPC method, which returns the deployed bytecode.

With this change the contract size using on-chain method and the build artifact method are equal.

fix: add edge case check for `testChainlogIntegrity`

Without this check, there would be a case where changes to the chainlog
not followed by a version bump would actually make the test pass.

Imagine this:

```
KEY A -> ADDRESS A
KEY B -> ADDRESS B
KEY C -> ADDRESS C
```

If the spell removes `KEY C` and adds `ANOTHER KEY` with `ADDRESS C` as a value, we'd get:

```
KEY A -> ADDRESS A
KEY B -> ADDRESS B
ANOTHER KEY -> ADDRESS C
```

This should cause a version update, but the test is not enforcing it.

Use brownie directly - issue 71 (#102)

Fixes #71 (use brownie directly). Also towards #103 (51% vesting wallet). All tests passing.

Brownie:
* aggressive use of brownie, to unlock its power. Root directory `./` is treated as a brownie project, and loaded as BROWNIE_PROJECT in `util/constants.py`
* README updated accordingly: compiling, how connecting to ganache works, the non-need to {deploy contracts up front, need for addresses up-front}. Docs could get streamlined more, but at least it's got the info now:)
* new `contracts/` directory
* copy ocean (v3) contracts from github.com/oceanprotocol/contracts into `contracts/oceanv3`. Only things changed so far are the openzeppelin imports, and one SPDX line added
* got oceanv3 working end-to-end. Super-thin utility module 'oceanv3util.py`. New test sub-directory adapted from `web3engine/test/test*.py`. engine, agents, tests updated accordingly. 
* Add `Simpletoken.sol` to have tokens without relying on oceanv3 datatokens. Use Simpletoken for OCEAN.
* remove intermediate directory `assets`, so that `netlists` and `agents` are at `./`. Simpler.
* remove `web3engine`, `web3tools`, because brownie renders them obsolete
* refactoring some `util/` files for simplicity. globaltokens, constants, etc.
* can specify logging interval -- how often to log to stdout and csv.
* add `brownie-config.yaml`, so that I can specify compiler version (needed to get oceanv3 to compile)

51% Vesting wallet:
* Add `contracts/VestingWallet.sol` and test sub-directory. It's based on OpenZeppelin VestingWallet.sol, but adapted to work an older solc compiler version (0.5.7). Ideally, we can revert those changes and be able to use its intended version (0.8.0), since then no changes needed and it reduces security risk.
* Implement `VestingFunderAgent`, `VestingWalletAgent`, `VestingBeneficiaryAgent`, and the netlist that uses them `netlists/scheduler/netlist.py`. End-to-end `tsp run` and `tsp plot` works on these, to generate plots.
* When engine takes a step of "time step", the chain also advances one block with that time step, via `chain.mine()`. This was critical for `VestingWallet.sol`, and for more future functionality too.

Known issues:
* when scheduler runs past the year 2038, it crashes. This is the y2038 problem. Parameters are currently set to stop beforehand. But this needs fixing for Ocean 51% work.
